hero.thesis: Launch and sell a Figma UI kit tailored for indie developers. Target: $39 one-time purchase, no support, scalable.
hero.marketSize: The global market for UI components is estimated to exceed $4 billion by 2025, with indie developers making up a significant segment.
hero.payingEvidence: Figma's user base has grown exponentially, with thousands of developers seeking ready-made UI kits on platforms like Gumroad and Whop.

actions.action: Identify 10 popular indie dev projects on platforms like ProductHunt and IndieHackers.
actions.success: List of 10 projects with themes and design needs.

actions.action: Survey 20 indie developers in the 'IndieDevHub' Discord about their UI needs.
Analyze survey data to determine common component needs.
Sketch low-fidelity wireframes for identified components.
Begin high-fidelity designs in Figma for 2 components.
Finish high-fidelity designs for remaining components.
Create a prototype in Figma to demo all components.
Test prototype with 5 indie developers for feedback.
Incorporate feedback and finalize designs.
actions.success: All feedback incorporated and designs finalized.

ninetyDay.title: Launch Preparation

actions.action: Set up a landing page on Webflow for the UI kit.
actions.success: Landing page live with product details and pricing.

actions.action: Create a Gumroad account and upload the UI kit.
actions.success: UI kit available for sale on Gumroad.

actions.action: Design promotional graphics for social media.

ninetyDay.metric: UI kit listed on Gumroad with live landing page.

actions.action: Announce the UI kit on 'IndieDevHub' Discord and ProductHunt.
Email 50 contacts from the 'IndieDevList' with promo details.
Post promotional graphics on Twitter and LinkedIn.
actions.success: Posts live on Twitter and LinkedIn.

actions.action: Collect feedback from first 10 customers via email.
Identify potential improvements based on feedback.
Implement quick wins from feedback into the UI kit.

ninetyDay.title: Expand Reach

actions.action: Reach out to 5 influencers in the design community for collaborations.
Write a guest post for 'DesignWeekly' blog about indie UI kits.
Create a short demo video for YouTube.

ninetyDay.metric: Guest post published and demo video live.

actions.action: Set up ConvertKit for email capture on landing page.
Create a 3-email welcome sequence for new subscribers.
A/B test landing page headline and CTA.
Identify 10 potential partners for affiliate marketing.
Reach out to identified partners with affiliate proposal.
Onboard 2 partners to affiliate program.
Plan a content calendar with 4 blog post topics.
actions.success: Content calendar ready for the month.

actions.action: Write and publish the first blog post.
Promote blog post on social media and forums.
Host a live Q&A on Instagram about the UI kit.
Create a monthly newsletter with updates and tips.
Offer a limited-time discount to boost end-of-month sales.

ninetyDay.title: Review and Reflect

actions.action: Analyze sales data and identify top-performing channels.
Review customer feedback and plan future updates.
Set goals for the next quarter based on insights.

ninetyDay.metric: Quarterly goals established with clear metrics.

toolStack.name: Figma
toolStack.why: Industry-standard design tool for UI kits.
toolStack.pricing: Free to start, $12/month for premium features.

toolStack.name: Gumroad
toolStack.why: Platform for selling digital products with ease.

toolStack.name: Webflow
toolStack.why: No-code tool for building a professional landing page.

toolStack.name: ConvertKit
toolStack.why: Email marketing tool for managing subscribers and sequences.
toolStack.pricing: Free for up to 300 subscribers, then $29/month.

toolStack.name: Calendly
toolStack.why: Scheduling live Q&A sessions with followers.

firstTenCustomers.script:
Hey [Name],

I'm launching a Figma UI kit designed specifically for indie developers like you. It features production-ready components to streamline your design process. No support needed, just $39 one-time. 

Check it out here: [Landing Page URL]

Let me know if you have any questions!

Best,
[Your Name]
firstTenCustomers.cadence: Initial email followed by a reminder 3 days later.

pricing.starter: $39 one-time purchase for the complete UI kit.
pricing.raisingRules: Consider raising the price by $10 after 100 sales.

failureModes: Ignoring market feedback: Leads to a product that doesn't sell.
Poor landing page design: Results in low conversion rates.
No marketing plan: Leads to zero sales.
Ignoring competition: Results in undifferentiated product.
Failing to update kit: Leads to customer dissatisfaction.
Poor follow-up with initial customers: Misses feedback loop.

setup.llc: Use Stripe Atlas to incorporate for $500 if needed.
setup.payments: Set up on Gumroad for seamless transactions.
setup.contracts: Not needed for a one-time digital product.

scaling: To scale beyond the initial hustle, consider hiring a part-time designer to expand your UI kit offerings. Use their skills to create themed kits, targeting different niches within the indie dev community. Automate your sales and marketing processes further by integrating n8n for workflow automation, ensuring you can handle increased customer inquiries and sales. Expand your reach by partnering with design influencers to create co-branded kits, offering them a revenue share on sales from their channels. As your revenue grows, consider hiring a community manager to handle customer engagement and feedback, freeing you to focus on product development and strategic partnerships.
